surface_terms: INTERMARKET DYNAMICS
Commodities move opposite to bond prices
If bonds move higher, commodities will move lower
Stocks move together with bonds
Stock traders should constantly refer to stock market indices and the bond market
The bond market serves as a key indicator
If bond prices are going higher, signifying lower interest rates
stocks like this environment, providing confidence for stock buyers due to underlying fundamental strength
If bonds are trading lower, meaning interest rate yields are increasing
stocks do not like higher interest rates
Bonds themselves do not like a high interest rate environment

When examining commodities
A strong dollar diminishes demand for grain and livestock/agricultural exports
US Dollar Index weakness instills increased demand for grain and agricultural exports

If the US Dollar Index is going higher or rallying, this is seen with stocks and bonds moving up
If the US Dollar Index moves lower, this is seen with stocks and bonds both trending lower as well
